rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim ('we present an adaptive MPC framework') is present but weakly framed; the gap statement and novelty claim ('current implementation differs in the specific update law') are vague enough that sharpening the thesis is part of any credible strengthening pass.
rationale.R1.2: The argument structure has two notable coherence problems: (1) the bandwidth parameter is justified by 'prior experience' without bridging back to the formal update law, and (2) the final admission that closed-loop stability was 'assumed rather than formally proven' directly undermines the preceding performance claims and needs logical reconciliation, not just hedging.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple result claims are weakly anchored to evidence: 'appears to be relatively robust,' 'we believe this represents an advantage,' and the '~23%' figure lacks variance or significance framing. Strengthening requires tightening these hedges to what the benchmark data actually supports.
rationale.R1.6: The prose has pervasive tense inconsistencies ('evaluated' / 'will reduce' / 'is tuned' / 'had implemented' mixed throughout), run-on sentences spanning multiple clauses, and passive-voice constructions that obscure agency. These are load-bearing: if unresolved, the prose remains weak regardless of other improvements.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'make this stronger' prioritize fixing the writing mechanics (prose quality, tense consistency, sentence structure), or hardening the scientific claims (tightening hedged language, addressing the unproven stability admission, anchoring the 23% result to statistical context)?
